author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii. 21, ‘How oft shall my brother sin against me,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii. 21, ‘How oft shall my brother sin against me,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yesterday the holy Gospel warned us not to neglect the sins of our brethren: “But if thy brother shall sin against thee, rebuke him between him and thee alone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if he shall refuse to hear thee, take with thee two or three more; that in the mouth of two or three witnesses, every word may be established.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if he shall neglect to hear them too, tell it to the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if he shall neglect to hear the Church, let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To-day also the section which follows, and which we heard when it was read, relates to the same subject.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the Lord Jesus had said this to Peter, he went on to ask his Master, how often he should forgive a brother who had sinned against him; and he enquired whether seven times would be enough.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Lord answered him, Not only seven times, but seventy times seven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then he added a parable very full of terror: That the “kingdom of heaven is like unto an householder, which took account with his servants; among whom he found one that owed ten thousand talents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when he commanded all that he had, and all his family, and himself to be sold, and the debt to be paid, he fell down at his lord’s feet,” and prayed for delay, and obtained entire remission.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as we have heard, “His lord was moved with compassion, and forgave him all the debt.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then that man free from his debt, but a bondslave of iniquity, after he had gone out from the presence of his lord, found in his turn a debtor of his own, who owed him, not ten thousand talents, the sum which had been remitted to him, but a hundred denarii; and “he began to drag him by the throat, and say, Pay me that thou owest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then he besought his fellow-servant as he had done his lord; but he did not find his fellow-servant such a man as the other had found his lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He not only would not forgive him the debt; but he did not even grant him a delay.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hurried him along with great violence to make him pay, he who had been but just now set free from his debt to his lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His fellow-servants were displeased; and “went and told their lord what was done;” and the lord summoned his servant to his presence, and said to him, “O thou wicked servant, when thou didst owe me so great a debt, in pity to thee I forgave thee all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellow-servant, even as I had pity on thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he commanded that all which he had forgiven him should be paid.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is then for our instruction that He put forth this parable, and by this warning He would save us from perishing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “So,” said He, “shall My heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, Brethren, the thing is plain, useful is the admonition, and a wholesome obedience is by all means due, that what hath been bidden may be fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For every man is at once God’s debtor, and hath also some brother a debtor to himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who is there who is not God’s debtor, but he in whom there can be found no sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who is there who hath not a brother his debtor, but he against whom no one hath sinned?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Think you that any one among mankind can be found, who is not himself bounden to his brother by some sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then every man is a debtor, yet having himself his own debtors too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The righteous God therefore appointeth a rule for thee toward thy debtor, which He also will observe with His.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For two works of mercy are there, which deliver us, which the Lord hath Himself briefly laid down in the Gospel: “Forgive, and ye shall be forgiven: give, and it shall be given unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive, and ye shall be forgiven,” relates to pardoning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give, and it shall be given unto you,” relates to doing kindnesses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As to what He saith of pardoning, thou both wishest thy sin to be pardoned thee, and thou hast another whom thou mayest pardon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, as to the doing kindnesses; a beggar asks of thee, and thou art God’s beggar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are all when we pray God’s beggars; we stand, yea rather we fall prostrate before the door of the Great Householder, we groan in supplication wishing to receive something; and this something is God Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does the beggar ask of thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what dost thou ask of God, but Christ, who saith, “I am the living Bread which came down from heaven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would you be forgiven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive, and it shall be forgiven you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would you receive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give, and it shall be given unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now hear what in so plain a precept I may cause a difficulty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this question of forgiveness when pardon is asked, and it is due from him who should grant it, it may be a difficulty to us as it was to Peter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How often ought I to forgive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is up to seven times sufficient?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “It is not sufficient,” saith the Lord, “I say not unto thee, Until seven times; but, Until seventy times seven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now reckon up how often thy brother hath sinned against thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou canst reach the seventy-eighth fault, so as to get beyond the seventy times seven, then set about revenge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is this then what He really means, and is it really so, that if he shall sin “seventy times seven,” thou shouldest forgive him; but if he shall sin seventy times and eight, it should then be lawful for thee not to forgive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay I am bold to say, that if he should even sin seventy-eight times, thou must forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, as I have said, if he shall sin seventy-eight times, forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if he sin a hundred times, forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why need I say, so and so often?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In one word, as often as he shall sin, forgive him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have I then taken upon me to overpass the measure of my Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He fixed the limit of forgiveness in the number seventy-seven; shall I presume to overleap this limit?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not so, I have not presumed to go at all beyond.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have heard the Lord Himself speaking in His Apostle where there is no measure or number fixed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He says, “Forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any, as God in Christ hath forgiven you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here you have the rule.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Christ have forgiven thee thy sins “seventy times and seven” only, if He have pardoned up to this point, and refused to pardon beyond it; then do thou also fix this limit, and be loth to forgive beyond it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if Christ hath found thousands of sins upon sins, and hath yet forgiven all; withdraw not then thy mercy, but ask the forgiveness of that large number.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it was not without a meaning that the Lord said “seventy times seven;” forasmuch as there is no trespass whatever which thou oughtest not to forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See this servant in the parable, who being a debtor was found to have a debtor, owed ten thousand talents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And I suppose that ten thousand talents are at least ten thousand sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I will not say how but one talent will include all sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how much did the other servant owe him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He owed a hundred denarii.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now is not this more than “seventy and seven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet the Lord was wroth, because he did not forgive him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For not only is a hundred more than “seventy-seven;” but a hundred denarii, perhaps are a thousand” asses.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what was this to ten thousand talents?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so let us be ready to forgive all the trespasses which are committed against us, if we desire to be forgiven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if we consider our sins, and reckon up what we do in deed, what by the eye, what by the ear, what by thought, what by numberless movements; I know not whether we so much as sleep without a talent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore do we daily beg, daily knock at the ears of God by prayer, daily prostrate ourselves and say, “Forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What debts of thine?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All, or a certain part?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt answer, All.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then do thou with thy debtor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then is the rule thou layest down, this the condition thou speakest of; this the covenant and agreement thou dost mention when thou prayest, saying, “Forgive us, as we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then, Brethren, is the meaning of “seventy times seven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear, for it is a great mystery, a wonderful sacrament.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Lord was baptized, the Evangelist St. Luke has in that place commemorated His generations in the regular order, series, and line in which they had come down to that generation in which Christ was born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Matthew begins at Abraham, and comes down to Joseph in a descending order; but Luke begins to reckon in an ascending order.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why does the one reckon in a descending, and the other in an ascending order?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because Matthew set forth the generation of Christ by which He came down to us; and so he began to reckon when Christ was born in a descending order.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas, because Luke begins to reckon when Christ was baptized; in this is the beginning of ascension, he begins to reckon in an ascending order, and in his reckoning he has completed seventy-seven generations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With whom did he begin his reckoning?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe with whom?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He began to reckon from Christ up to Adam himself, who was the first sinner, and who begat us with the bond of sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He reckoned up to Adam, and so there are reckoned seventy-seven generations; that is, from Christ up to Adam and from Adam up to Christ are the aforesaid seventy-seven generations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then if no generation was omitted, there is no exemption of any trespass which ought not to be forgiven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For therefore did he reckon up his seventy-seven generations, which number the Lord mentioned as to the forgiveness of sins; since he begins to reckon from the baptism, wherein all sins are remitted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, Brethren, observe in this a yet greater mystery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the number seventy-seven is a mystery of the remission of sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So many are the generations found to be from Christ to Adam.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then, ask with somewhat more careful diligence for the secret meaning of this number, and enquire into its hidden meaning; with more careful diligence knock, that it may be opened unto thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Righteousness consists in the observance of the Law of God: true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Law is set forth in ten precepts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore it was that the servant in the parable “owed ten thousand talents.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is that memorable Decalogue written by the finger of God, and delivered to the people by Moses, the servant of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He “owed” then “ten thousand talents;” which signifies all sins, with reference to the number of the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the other “owed a hundred denarii;” derived equally from the same number.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a hundred times a hundred make ten thousand; and ten times ten make a hundred.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the one “owed ten thousand talents,” and the other ten times ten denarii.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there was no departure from the number of the law, and in both numbers you will find every kind of sin included.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both are debtors, and both implore and beg for pardon; but the wicked, ungrateful servant would not repay what he had received, would not grant the mercy which had been undeservedly accorded to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider then, Brethren; every man begins from Baptism; he goes out free, the “ten thousand talents” are forgiven him; and when he goes out, he will soon find some fellow-servant his debtor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him note then, what sin itself is; for the number eleven is the transgression of the law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the law is ten, sin eleven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the law is denoted by ten, sin by eleven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is sin denoted by eleven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because to get to eleven, there is the transgression of the ten.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the due limit is fixed in the law; and the transgression of it is sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when you have passed beyond the ten, you come to eleven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This high mystery was figured out when the tabernacle was commanded to be built.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are many things mentioned there in number, which are a great mystery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Among the rest, curtains of haircloth were ordered to be made not ten, but eleven; because by haircloth is signified the confession of sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what do you require more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would you know how that all sins are contained in this number “seventy-seven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seven then is usually put for a whole; because in seven days the revolution of time is completed, and when the seventh is ended, it returns to the first again, that the same revolution may be continued.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In such revolutions whole ages pass away: yet there is no departure from the number seven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He spoke of all sins, when He said “seventy times seven;” for multiply that eleven seven times, and it makes seventy-seven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore would He have all sins forgiven, for He marked them out by the number” seventy-seven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one then retain against himself by refusing to forgive, lest it be retained against him, when he prayeth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God saith, “Forgive, and thou shalt be forgiven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I have forgiven thee first; do thou at least forgive after that.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou wilt not forgive, I will call thee back, and put upon thee again all that I had remitted to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Truth doth not speak falsely; Christ neither deceiveth, nor is deceived, and He hath said at the close of the parable, “So likewise shall your Father which is in heaven do unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou findest a Father, imitate thy Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou wilt not imitate Him, thou art devising to be disinherited.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “So likewise” then “shall My heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say not with the tongue, “I forgive,” and put off to forgive in the heart; for by His threat of vengeance God showeth thee thy punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God knoweth where thou speakest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man can hear thy voice; God looketh into thy conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou sayest, I forgive; forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Better is it that thou shouldest be violent in words, and forgive in the heart, than in words be soft, and in the heart relentless.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then unruly boys will beg, and take it hard to be beat taking exception against us when we wish to chastise them after this fashion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have sinned, but forgive me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well, I have forgiven, and he sins again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive me,” he cries, and I have forgiven him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He sins a third time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive me,” he cries, and a third time I have forgiven him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then the fourth time let him be beat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he will say, “What! have I tired you out to seventy-seven times?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if by such exceptions the severity of discipline sleep, upon the suppression of discipline wickedness will rage with impunity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is to be done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us reprove with words, and if need be with scourges; but let us withal forgive the sin, and cast away the remembrance of it from the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For therefore did the Lord add, “from your hearts,” that though through affection discipline be exercised, gentleness might not depart out of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is so kind and gentle as the surgeon with his knife?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that is to be cut cries, yet cut he is; he that is to be cauterized cries, but cauterized he is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is not cruelty; on no account let that surgeon’s treatment be called cruelty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cruel he is against the wounded part that the patient may be cured; for if the wound be softly dealt with, the man is lost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus then would I advise, my Brethren, that we love our brethren, howsoever they may have sinned against us; that we let not affection toward them depart out of our hearts, and that when need is, we exercise discipline toward them; lest by the relaxation of discipline, wickedness increase, and we begin to be accused on God’s behalf, for it has been read to us, “Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Certainly, if one, as is the only true way, distinguishes the times, and so solves the question, all is true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the sin be in secret, rebuke it in secret.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the sin be public and open, rebuke it publicly that the sinner may be reformed; and “that others also may fear.”
